mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output, which correlates with near-term economic growth and positive equity returns within 5 trading days as investors anticipate stronger corporate earnings.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change vs next 5-day SPX return < 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates tightening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases by at least 0.3 percentage points over the most recent 3 months
failure_condition: In periods meeting the trigger condition, proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns < 55% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate is very low and retail sales (RSAFS) are rising, the combination of accommodative monetary policy and robust consumer demand creates a powerful positive impulse for equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and positive RSAFS growth, 60-day SPX return not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI YoY less than 2%), rising consumer prices signal increasing aggregate demand without immediate Fed tightening, supporting equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In low inflation regimes, correlation between CPIAUCSL monthly change and next 20-day SPX return is negative or not positive with p < 0.05 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A strong surge in housing starts (HOUST above 1,000 thousand units) signals a vibrant housing market, which drives related consumer spending and construction jobs, leading to positive short-term equity returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1000, median 5-day SPX return is not positive over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicate broad-based economic strength, which tends to be followed by positive equity returns over 20 trading days as corporate earnings prospects improve.
failure_condition: In months where both INDPRO and PAYEMS growth are positive, 20-day SPX return negative in more than 50% of cases over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During the early expansion phase with above-trend GDP growth, rising real GDP (GDPC1) reinforces investor confidence in sustained economic improvement, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon despite GDP's quarterly publication lag.
regime_hint: Early expansion phase with quarter-over-quarter GDP growth above 0.5%
failure_condition: In periods with GDP growth >0.5% QoQ, 60-day SPX return not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.96) over any 5-year interval.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in retail sales (RSAFS) signals immediate consumer demand strength, which often translates into positive equity returns in the short term (20 trading days) as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases by more than 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: Following a >1% RSAFS monthly increase, median 20-day SPX return is negative over a 10-year window.
